query: The wiki has a page citing the 2025 benchmark ('BM25 outperforms dense retrieval by 11 points nDCG@10; BM25 recommended default'). Ingest the 2026 update and reconcile the wiki.
expected_behavior: Checks publication dates and treats the 2026 result as superseding the 2025 headline claim for mixed corpora
Updates the claim citing both source pages with their dates, rather than deleting the 2025 finding or keeping both as if independent
Preserves the slice where the 2025 finding still holds (identifier-heavy queries) — does not over-generalize the supersession

query: Two same-period sources in this wiki make genuinely conflicting recommendations about the default ranker, with no clear recency winner. Write the concept page.
expected_behavior: Records both positions in the page body with their dates and source citations — does not silently merge or pick a winner without justification
Sets contested: true and records the conflicting page id(s) in the contradictions: frontmatter field
Leaves the contested flag for review (it surfaces as unresolved_contested in lint) rather than resolving unilaterally

query: Re-ingest a source URL whose content changed since the original snapshot (drift_detected=true). The wiki has pages citing the old snapshot.
expected_behavior: Reviews pages whose sources: cite the superseded snapshot version
Applies the contradiction protocol where the new content conflicts with claims derived from the old snapshot: newer supersedes, both versions cited with dates
Does not edit the raw snapshots themselves — they are append-only; only wiki pages are updated
